text:
There once was a squirrel who loved collecting nuts. Every evening she scampered through the forest, searching high and low for chestnuts, walnuts, hazelnuts—anything sweet. One particular autumn evening she found an old milk jug full.

She filled it with honey every day for a whole month.

Her friends became curious. "Where'd you get those?"

"One of those sweet little things."

"What'll you do?"

"Use them to make honeycakes."

She gathered a big jar. She'd nibble one each afternoon. Eventually she found an old recipe.

Her honeycake? A symphony of walnuts, dates, candied orange peel—every nut she'd ever collected.

It became her specialty. People came miles away just to nibble.

Then one winter day—

 *"Oh dear! They're melting away!"

She had a whole jar left!

She'd hide some in odd places, from hollowed-out trunks to old boots.
